ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general iced tea class (FOODON_03311737). No specific entry exists for lime-shiso iced tea; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 AN0KL covers tea-based beverages broadly. No specific code for lime-shiso iced tea exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept exists for lime shiso iced tea. General beverage concepts are not sufficiently specific to warrant assignment.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 300 ml (one cup or tall glass), representing a typical single-serve portion as sold at Co Chung Restaurant in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, tea, ready-to-drink, unsweetened (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy); lime juice raw (USDA FoodData Central); perilla/shiso leaf (USDA FoodData Central)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry exists for lime-shiso iced tea in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated by component assembly: unsweetened brewed tea base (~0 kcal/100 g), lime juice contribution (~3–5 kcal/100 g at typical dilution ~10% v/v), and shiso leaf infusion (negligible macro contribution at typical infusion levels). Sugar syrup is listed as optional per the restaurant description; nutrients are calculated for the unsweetened base version. Per-100 g values reflect the diluted ready-to-drink beverage without added sugar syrup.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — University of Sydney GI Database; unsweetened tea and lemon/lime beverages reference
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for lime-shiso iced tea. Unsweetened tea and citrus-infused water beverages are consistently reported at GI < 5 in the University of Sydney GI Database due to negligible glycemic carbohydrate content. GI is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel) per Project Veritas convention for essentially zero-GI beverages. Glycemic load per 300 ml serving is 0.0 g because available carbohydrate per serving is approximately 3 g, which at GI 1 yields GL = (1 × 3) / 100 = 0.03, rounded to 0.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~2 mg/100 g); no added salt
hypertension.guidance: With approximately 6 mg sodium per 300 ml serving (unsweetened), this beverage poses negligible sodium load and is suitable for individuals managing hypertension; however, patients should request the no-sugar-syrup option to avoid any indirect cardiovascular risk from excess sugar intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible glycemic carbohydrate in the unsweetened base; optional sugar syrup adds ~25.5 g sugar per 30 ml
type2_diabetes.guidance: The unsweetened version of this beverage has a glycemic index of effectively zero and a glycemic load of 0.0, making it appropriate for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; patients must decline the optional sugar syrup (30 ml ≈ 25.5 g sugar) to avoid a significant postprandial glucose spike.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat and cholesterol; lime provides vitamin C; shiso leaf contains rosmarinic acid with antioxidant properties
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The unsweetened lime-shiso iced tea is cardiovascular-neutral with no saturated fat, no cholesterol, and trace vitamin C from lime juice; shiso leaf polyphenols (rosmarinic acid) may offer mild antioxidant benefit, though clinical evidence at typical beverage concentrations remains limited.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Homemade Lime Shiso Leaf Iced Tea (Vietnamese: Trà Chanh Lá Tía Tô) is a refreshing cold beverage served at Co Chung Restaurant in Singapore, reflecting the Vietnamese culinary tradition of herb-infused iced drinks. The drink is prepared by steeping fresh shiso leaves (tía tô, also known as perilla or Perilla frutescens) in chilled or room-temperature water, combining the infusion with freshly squeezed lime juice, and serving over ice. The result is a lightly herbaceous, citrus-forward beverage with a subtle anise-mint character from the shiso leaf and a bright tartness from the lime. The colour is typically pale green to light amber. Sugar syrup is offered as an optional add-on (30 ml ≈ 25.5 g sugar); the unsweetened base version is essentially calorie-free at approximately 4 kcal per 100 g. Nutritionally, the unsweetened beverage contributes negligible macronutrients — protein approximately 0.1 g/100 g, fat 0.0 g/100 g, carbohydrate approximately 1.0 g/100 g — along with trace vitamin C from lime juice and minor potassium from both lime and shiso. The glycemic index is effectively zero (set to 1 per convention) and the glycemic load per serving is 0.0, making it highly suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome when consumed without the sugar syrup. Shiso leaf is rich in rosmarinic acid, luteolin, and apigenin, which are polyphenols with documented antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties in vitro, though clinical significance at beverage-infusion concentrations is uncertain. The drink is vegan, gluten-free, dairy-free, and halal-compatible. It is commonly consumed as a light refreshment during warm weather and is culturally associated with Vietnamese herb-forward beverage traditions transplanted into the Singapore food scene.
common_user_queries: What is Trà Chanh Lá Tía Tô?
Is lime shiso iced tea good for diabetics?
How many calories in lime shiso iced tea?

What does shiso leaf taste like in tea?
Is the sugar syrup included in the nutrition?
What is tía tô in English?

confidence_reason: No direct entry for lime-shiso iced tea exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or any primary regional nutrition database. Nutrient values are estimated by component assembly from USDA FoodData Central reference values for unsweetened tea, lime juice, and perilla leaf at typical beverage dilution ratios. GI is estimated by analogy with unsweetened citrus-herb teas from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level evidence is strong and the beverage is predominantly water with trace macronutrients, leaving limited room for significant estimation error in the unsweetened version.
notes: 1. Nutrient values represent the unsweetened base version (no sugar syrup). If 30 ml sugar syrup is added per the restaurant description, energy increases by approximately 85 kcal per serving and carbohydrate increases by approximately 25.5 g per serving, which would substantially alter the glycemic load and diabetic suitability. 2. fat_g is naturally zero as this is a water-based herbal infusion containing no lipid-contributing ingredients at typical preparation ratios. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and cholesterol_mg are confirmed zero for the same reason. 3. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as the shiso leaves are infused and removed; no solid plant matter is consumed. 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is naturally zero as this is a plant-based beverage with no animal-derived ingredients. 5. vitamin_d_mcg is naturally zero as no vitamin D sources are present in this beverage. 6. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used for estimation; shiso leaf does contain beta-carotene but concentration in the infused beverage is not reliably quantifiable from available sources. 7. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and selenium_mcg are -1 as these are not reported or reliably estimable for the diluted beverage infusion from available approved sources. 8. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates in the unsweetened form; gi_category low per Project Veritas convention for essentially zero-GI beverages. 9. FoodOn FOODON_03311737 (iced tea) used as closest_parent; no specific FoodOn class exists for lime-shiso iced tea. FoodEx2 AN0KL (tea-based beverages) used as closest_parent. 10. Price from field survey data listing: SGD 8.68 (single restaurant, Co Chung Restaurant — Lorong Telok). Price data is preserved from input but not used in nutritional calculations.
